editor_settings.title: ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 Unboxing: Is This Monster GPU Still Worth Buying?
editor_settings.content:
ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 Unboxing: Is This Monster GPU Still Worth Buying?

The ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 still looks ridiculous in the best way.

Huge cooler. Premium box. That heavy “this is not a normal GPU” feeling.

But in 2026, the real question is not whether it looks amazing. The real question is whether it is still smart to buy.

The Unboxing Is Half The Appeal

The TikTok from ASUS ROG is basically pure GPU theater: Strix branding, RTX 4090, ASMR unboxing energy, and the kind of presentation that makes PC builders pause scrolling.

@_asusrog We all love the sound! 😍 #ROG #Strix #RTX4090 #ASMR #unboxing #VGA #gaming ♬ original sound - ROG Global

That is why the ROG Strix line works so well online. It does not just sell specs. It sells the feeling of building the no-compromise PC.

And yes, the Strix RTX 4090 still has that feeling.

What Makes The Strix RTX 4090 Special?

The RTX 4090 itself is already extreme: NVIDIA lists it with 24GB of GDDR6X memory, Ada Lovelace architecture, DLSS support, and serious 4K gaming power.

The Strix version adds the premium ASUS angle: bigger cooler, more aggressive design, strong power delivery, and the kind of oversized build people buy when they want silence and headroom.

This is not the card for a tiny case. It is not the card for a budget build. It is the card for someone who wants the GPU to be the centerpiece.

Who Should Still Buy It?

Buy the ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 if:

  • You play at 4K and want high settings.
  • You care about ray tracing and DLSS.
  • You do Blender, editing, rendering, or local AI work.
  • You need 24GB of VRAM.
  • You have the case space and power supply for it.

Do not buy it just because it looks cool. That is how expensive regrets happen.

The 2026 Problem

The problem is simple: RTX 5090 exists now.

If a used or new Strix RTX 4090 is priced close to an RTX 5090, the 4090 stops being a clean recommendation. You have to compare live pricing, warranty, condition, and your actual workload.

If the Strix is meaningfully cheaper, it can still be a beast. If it is barely cheaper, I would move newer.

For current top-end RTX shopping, compare against live 5090 options too:

[product-card:asus-tuf-gaming-oc-geforce-rtx-5090-32gb-graphics-card-32gb-gddr7-pcie-50-21760-cuda-cores-8k-support-triple-fan-cooling]


Final Verdict

The ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 is still one of the most desirable RTX 4090 cards ever made.

It is big, premium, powerful, and perfect for people who want a serious 4K or creator build.

But do not pay collector-level money for it in 2026. Buy it only when the price is clearly better than the newer high-end RTX alternatives.

Sources Checked

  • NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 official page
  • NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 official page
  • ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 specs
  • Gigabyte AORUS RTX 4090 Master product page
